pageHero.title: AI-Aware Interview Tips
pageHero.subtitle: How to prepare for interviews where AI tools are allowed (and expected).

coreShift.title: The Core Shift
coreShift.text: Interviewers now observe how you work WITH AI. The skill isn’t using AI — it’s knowing when AI output is wrong, incomplete, or unnecessarily complex. You’re evaluated on problem-solving, code quality, and verification, NOT prompt engineering.

items.text: Solve problems with Copilot/Claude, verify and debug AI suggestions
Practice prompting AI for well-defined subtasks (not end-to-end solving)
Learn to spot common AI mistakes: off-by-one, wrong edge cases, overcomplicated solutions
Design systems with LLM components: embeddings, vector DBs, RAG pipelines
Practice explaining your reasoning out loud while using AI tools

dos: Use AI as a productivity booster for well-defined subtasks
Always read and verify AI-generated code before accepting
Explain trade-offs in AI suggestions to the interviewer
Show you understand the code AI generates
Use AI for boilerplate, then customize and optimize
donts: Don’t use AI as an end-to-end solver — that’s not what they’re testing
Don’t accept AI output without reviewing it
Don’t use AI tools in interviews that don’t explicitly allow them
Don’t rely on AI for your problem-solving reasoning
Don’t use hidden AI tools (earbuds, second screen) — it’s cheating

ethicalWarning.title: Ethical Boundary
ethicalWarning.text: Only use AI during interviews when explicitly allowed. Using AI secretly is considered cheating and can result in permanent bans from companies. Use AI for preparation, not deception.

links.text: Canva: Use AI in Our Interviews
